node.name: Node.js
node.description: Official OpenClaw runtime already bundles Node.js 24 and Corepack-managed pnpm.

python.name: Python
python.description: Adds Python 3, pip, venv, and pipx for automation, scraping, and data processing.

go.name: Go
go.description: Adds a pinned Go toolchain for backend, agent tools, and high-performance services.

rust.name: Rust
rust.description: Adds a minimal Rust toolchain through rustup.

cxx.name: C++ Toolchain
cxx.description: Adds build-essential, clang, cmake, ninja, gdb, and pkg-config.

profiles.name: Node.js Developer
profiles.summary: Fastest path for JavaScript and TypeScript skills, bots, and integrations.
recommendedFor: Teams who want the smallest delta from the official image

profiles.name: Python Developer
profiles.summary: Adds Python-first automation tooling for ETL, scripts, and lightweight data workflows.
recommendedFor: Automation-heavy users who mostly live in Python
Quick scripts, lightweight ETL, and notebook-adjacent tasks
Teams that still need OpenClaw's default Node runtime intact

profiles.name: Go Developer
profiles.summary: Lean Go-focused image for backend tools, daemons, and agent-side services.
recommendedFor: Teams that want a Go toolchain without extra language surface
Users who already know they do not need Python by default

profiles.name: Go + Python Developer
profiles.summary: Best fit for Go developers who still rely on Python tooling in real projects.
recommendedFor: Go engineers who regularly shell out to Python tools
Information processing, pipelines, and hybrid service stacks
Teams that want one image for both Go apps and Python helpers

profiles.name: Node.js + Python Collector
profiles.summary: The fastest choice for information collection, scraping, and content processing.
recommendedFor: Crawler pipelines with JS-based orchestration and Python parsing
Users who want the broadest automation surface with minimal setup

profiles.name: Rust + C++ Minimal
profiles.summary: Slim Rust image with a built-in C++ toolchain for native builds and FFI-heavy work.
recommendedFor: Rust engineers who need a focused native development image
Teams that want the slimmest curated profile while keeping native build tools
